pubmed_links: [Fujita et al. (2019) DYNC2H1 variants associated with hypothalamic hamartoma](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31197031/);[Green et al. (2022) Sporadic hypothalamic hamartoma is a ciliopathy with somatic and bi-allelic DYNC2H1 contributions](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35137044/)
summary: DYNC2H1 encodes the heavy chain of cytoplasmic dynein-2, essential for retrograde intraflagellar transport in primary cilia and Sonic Hedgehog signaling during brain development. Bi-allelic pathogenic variants (germline plus somatic) in DYNC2H1 cause sporadic hypothalamic hamartoma, a congenital brain lesion associated with high rates of autism (~19%), drug-resistant epilepsy, and intellectual disability, implicating ciliary dysfunction as a mechanism in neurodevelopmental conditions. (emerging evidence)
